sutta: thus have i heard once the memorable mahajunda lived among the chaitis at sahajati and there he spoke to the monk saying monks reverences prevent sir they rejoined and the verbal mahajunda said there are some monks dharma zealots who blame meditators saying these fellows say we are meditators we are meditators they meditate and are meditated and what do they meditate about what's good of them meditating how do they meditate there neither the dhamma zealots nor the meditator monks are pleased nor have they fared onwards for the wheel of many folk for their happiness their good not for the wheel and happiness of devas and men i'll just stop here for a while here the verbal mahajanda is saying that there are some monks who are what we call scholar manga and they criticize meditator monks and here the word meditate meditator is actually this word jayati or jayanti literally means to burn to burn and another meaning is uh breitla in candisons and that is the word now that is used for jhana jana is a state now where the mind becomes 1 pointed mind becomes brighter so in the early days of the buddha of buddhism during the buddha's time meditation was synonymous to cultivating jhana that is why the word jayathi is actually means to cultivate jhana that is why from the from this you can understand why the in early the early stages of buddhism in china they had this meditation school now which was called the chanchong hanchong is the zen school and this chan the chinese word chan comes from the word channel it's a abbreviation of channel and channel is the bollywood jana so in early stages of buddhism meditation is plainly synonymous with cultivating jhana and to continue and there are some monks meditators who blame dharma zealots saying these fellows say we are dharma zeelots we are dharma zealots they are puffed up proud excitable fellows mouthy speechifiers forgetful of mindfulness lacking self-possession and composure with their thoughts a wonder and their senses uncontrolled and what is their dharma zeal what goods their dharmasi how are they zealous in dhamma there too neither the meditators nor the dhammazilots are pleased nor have they fared onwards for the wheel of many folk and they had some dhamma zealots who just praised dharma zealots who pray is not meditators there too neither dumbass nor meditators are pleased and so neither are pleased nor have they fared onwards for the wheel of many folk for their happiness their good for the wheel and happiness of devas and men where forces you should train yourselves thus in being dharma zealots we will praise meditators thus should you train yourselves references and why verily sirs these wondrous persons are hardly found in the world they who live with the whole being in tune with the deafness and you should train yourselves thus provinces being meditators we will praise dharma zealots thus should you train yourselves references and why verily sirs these wondrous persons are hardly found in the world they who with inside penetrate and see the deep way of the goal that's the end of the sutta
commentary: in this utah you find the scholar monks blaming the criticizing the meditator monks and meditative monks sometimes criticize the scholarly monks but if a manga is a well-developed manga he should both be a scholar monk as well as a meditator monk you can see here many of the buddhas are hansa they knew the suttas very well and yet they were good in their meditation so that they became our hands so now since time immemorial people have been criticizing each other even the buddha was criticized by external ascetics and the buddha also decides external aesthetics but in the buddha's teachings the buddha thought that we should criticize wrong teachings in order to point out the true teachings however we should not get personal so we should um criticize the wrong teachings to point out the true teachings but we should not get personal and criticize persons only the teachings because if we get personal then anger arises and unwholesome states of mind arise and there will be a lot of problems in the sangha now we come to the next suta
